title: Review: vault refactor + eighth-round fixes
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — Two structural issues: (1) put() 404→NotFound will generate user confusion on misconfigured mounts; (2) test guards on path-validation tests are factually wrong and cause silent skipping in CI without VAULT_TOKEN. Both are fixable quickly.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) map_client_error 404 behavior for put is untested against a real Vault with a bad mount path — I'm inferring the 404 trigger from Vault API docs, not observed behavior; (2) no visibility into what vaultrs actually sends on the wire for the no-field put, so JSON re-serialization fidelity is unverified. Would raise confidence if: integration test for put to nonexistent mount confirming the error variant, and a test that round-trips a JSON object with non-ASCII keys.
